title: Scale CloudNative-PG PostgreSQL to 3 Replicas with PgBouncer and Automated Backups
subtasks: Update CloudNative-PG Cluster CR spec.instances from 2 to 3 for full HA (1 primary + 2 replicas)
Enable PgBouncer connection pooling by adding spec.pgbouncer section to CNPG Cluster CR: poolMode=transaction, defaultPoolSize=20, maxClientConnections=200
Verify streaming replication across all 3 instances via pg_stat_replication query
Configure automated daily base backups via CNPG ScheduledBackup CR: schedule '0 2 * * *', barmanObjectStore targeting sigma1-wal-archive R2 bucket
Verify WAL archiving is continuous (check barman WAL archive lag < 5 minutes)
Configure backup retention policy: 7 daily backups, 4 weekly backups
Test failover by deleting the primary pod and verifying automatic promotion of a replica within 30 seconds
Update sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap to use the PgBouncer service endpoint instead of direct postgres-rw endpoint
Run pgbench baseline test to verify connection pooling reduces connection overhead under load

title: Scale Valkey (Redis) to Sentinel HA with 3 Replicas
subtasks: Update Opstree Redis CR to enable Redis Sentinel mode with 3 replicas (1 master + 2 replicas + 3 sentinels)
Configure maxmemory policy as allkeys-lru with maxmemory set to 80% of pod memory limit
Update the sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap VALKEY_SIGMA1_URL to use the Sentinel-aware connection string (sentinel://...)
Verify Sentinel is monitoring the master and both replicas via SENTINEL MASTER and SENTINEL REPLICAS commands
Test automatic failover by killing the master pod and verifying Sentinel promotes a replica within 30 seconds
Verify all application services reconnect to the new master without manual intervention
Configure Valkey persistence with AOF appendonly yes and appendfsync everysec for durability
Add Prometheus redis-exporter sidecar for Valkey metrics (connected_clients, used_memory, keyspace_hits/misses)

title: Configure TLS Certificates via cert-manager with Let's Encrypt
subtasks: Verify cert-manager CRDs are installed in the cluster (or install cert-manager via Helm if not present)
Create a ClusterIssuer CR for Let's Encrypt production using Cloudflare DNS01 challenge solver with Cloudflare API token from sigma1-cloudflare-secret
Create a ClusterIssuer CR for Let's Encrypt staging for non-production testing
Create Certificate CRs for chat.sigma1.com and signals.sigma1.com domains
Verify certificates are issued and stored as Kubernetes TLS Secrets
Configure cloudflared tunnel to use the TLS secrets for origin server certificates (if origin TLS is required)
Create an internal CA ClusterIssuer for mTLS between internal services if Cilium encryption is not sufficient
Set up certificate renewal alerting: PrometheusRule for cert expiry < 14 days

title: Configure Cloudflare CDN for Static Assets and API Caching
subtasks: Configure Cloudflare CDN caching rules for R2 bucket static assets (equipment images, social media crops): Cache-Control max-age=86400, stale-while-revalidate=3600
Set up Cloudflare Page Rules or Cache Rules for /api/v1/catalog/* GET endpoints: cache with 5-minute TTL, bypass cache on Authorization header present
Configure Cloudflare Transform Rules to add security headers: Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options
Enable Cloudflare WAF managed rules for the sigma1.com domain
Configure Cloudflare rate limiting at the edge: 200 requests/minute per IP for API endpoints, 1000 requests/minute for static assets
Set up Cloudflare custom hostname for chat.sigma1.com with Full (strict) SSL mode
Enable Cloudflare HTTP/3 and Early Hints for the website domain
Configure Cloudflare R2 custom domain for direct static asset serving (e.g., cdn.sigma1.com)

title: Configure Network Policies for Default-Deny Cross-Namespace Isolation
subtasks: Create a default-deny CiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicy for all sigma1, databases, openclaw, and signal namespaces denying all ingress and egress by default
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy allowing DNS egress (port 53 UDP/TCP to kube-dns) for all pods in sigma1, databases, openclaw, and signal namespaces
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy for databases namespace: postgres pods accept ingress only from sigma1 namespace pods on port 5432; valkey pods accept ingress only from sigma1 namespace pods on port 6379
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy for signal namespace: signal-cli pod accepts ingress only from openclaw namespace (morgan) on port 7583
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy allowing Prometheus scraping: all pods accept ingress from monitoring namespace on /metrics port
Verify all existing per-service CiliumNetworkPolicies from Task 10 still function correctly with the new default-deny baseline
Test that cross-namespace traffic not explicitly allowed is dropped by attempting curl from arbitrary pods
Document the complete network policy matrix in ops/docs/network-policy-matrix.md

title: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector with Tracing and Metrics Pipeline
subtasks: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector as a DaemonSet in the monitoring namespace using the opentelemetry-collector Helm chart
Configure OTLP receiver on ports 4317 (gRPC) and 4318 (HTTP) for trace and metric ingestion from all services
Configure tail-based sampling processor: sample 100% of error traces, 10% of successful traces with latency > 200ms, 1% of all other traces
Configure batch processor with send_batch_size=512 and timeout=5s for efficient export
Configure Datadog exporter to forward traces and metrics to the existing Datadog Agent in the datadog-agent namespace
Configure Prometheus exporter for OpenTelemetry metrics, scraped by the existing monitoring stack
Create a ClusterIP Service otel-collector.monitoring.svc.cluster.local for service discovery by application pods
Update all service Deployment manifests to set OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://otel-collector.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:4317 environment variable
Verify traces flow end-to-end: send a test request through equipment-catalog and confirm trace appears in Datadog APM

title: Configure Prometheus Alert Rules for SLO Violations
subtasks: Create PrometheusRule CR for equipment-catalog latency SLO: alert when histogram_quantile(0.99, http_request_duration_seconds) > 0.5 for 5 minutes
Create PrometheusRule CR for all services error rate SLO: alert when rate(http_requests_total{status=~'5..'}[5m]) / rate(http_requests_total[5m]) > 0.01 for 2 minutes
Create PrometheusRule CR for pod availability: alert when kube_deployment_status_replicas_available < kube_deployment_spec_replicas for 5 minutes
Create PrometheusRule CR for PostgreSQL replication lag: alert when pg_replication_lag_seconds > 30 for 5 minutes
Create PrometheusRule CR for Valkey memory: alert when redis_memory_used_bytes / redis_memory_max_bytes > 0.9 for 10 minutes
Create PrometheusRule CR for Morgan chat response latency: alert when histogram_quantile(0.95, morgan_chat_response_seconds) > 10 for 5 minutes
Create PrometheusRule CR for certificate expiry: alert when certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds - time() < 1209600 (14 days)
Configure Alertmanager routing to send critical alerts to a dedicated Slack/Signal channel and high-severity alerts to email
Create Grafana dashboards: per-service request rate/error rate/latency heatmap, PostgreSQL connections and replication lag, Valkey memory and hit rate, CNPG backup status
